description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the python:3.7.2-stretch Docker image. Define variables AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=eu-central-1 and S3_BUCKET=aws-lambda-price-grabber and four stages: build, test, package, deploy. In the build stage add a build job that runs `echo \"Building\"` then `pip3 install -r requirements.txt -t source/` and stores the source/ directory as an artifact. In the test stage add a test job that runs `echo \"Testing\"` then `pip3 install -r requirements_unit_test.txt`, copies the source files with `cp -R source/* .`, and executes `nose2 -v`. In the package stage add a package job that runs `apt-get update`, `apt-get -y install zip`, changes into the source directory with `cd source`, creates a zip archive using `zip -r source.zip *`, and publishes the source/ directory as an artifact. In the deploy stage add a deploy job that installs awscli with `pip3 install awscli`, uploads `source/source.zip` and `source/website-monitor-list.yml` to `s3://$S3_BUCKET/` via `aws s3 cp`, runs `aws cloudformation package --template-file ./deployment/aws-create-lambda.json --s3-bucket $S3_BUCKET --output-template template-export.yml`, then `aws cloudformation deploy --template-file template-export.yml --stack-name aws-lambda-price-grabber-stack --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM --no-fail-on-empty-changeset`, and finally updates two Lambda functions with `aws lambda update-function-code --function-name grab-price --s3-bucket $S3_BUCKET --s3-key source.zip` and `aws lambda update-function-code --function-name grab-invoke --s3-bucket $S3_BUCKET --s3-key source.zip`, publishing ./template-export.yml as an artifact.
